title: eval_exists_test returns false for empty arg list — not vacuously true
description: eval_exists_test tracks found_any_name and returns it at the end, so an empty argument list returns false rather than true. Vacuous truth (true when no headers are named) would mean 'exists []' always passes, which is semantically wrong: the test is asking whether named headers exist, and with no names the question is meaningless — failing safe is correct. RFC 5228 §5.4 implies the test checks for the listed header names; zero names = zero to check = false is the right call.
